user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
i have a long-term portfolio for more than a decade.
it keeps printing. its convexity is changing. most of the risk is hedged out.

intraday trading was always a side source of income for me.
there are weeks when i don't even trade.
on a week, 3 trades, maybe 4 if the setups are clear, but not 5 trades per day?! wtf...

3-4 trades/week.
and then i can make a 300-500% portfolio growth (on the daytrader account... it is separated from teh longer-term one)
and then, i don't trade maybe for a week or two... depends... or make only one trade, when i see and "feel" the setup

I always did it this way. and this is how i gained my first 7 digit bank account balance before turned 30

and don't forget that i worked hard beside this, saved a lot of money, didn't bought designer luxury craps or anything (also i live alone, no family that is also makes it a bit more easier, but also harder bcs i pay everything on my own, no partner to help)

comment.body:
HI Alma  -- these levels are all SPX spot, right:

Downside
Pivot: 6639 → shifts to 6623 after 1PM.
If broken → 6590 = magnetic zone.
6557 = strongest support cluster.
Bias: very hard to break 6639/6623. If it does → market wants lower.

Upside
Pivot: 6675.
Above → pin to 6685, test 6698/6700, fade likely after 12:30PM.
AM magnet = 6715.

comment.body:
Happy for him and looks like inheritance from his grandmother who passed away. Makes sense that this is stock and not options and a one year hold . Weighing machine works against intraday traders or swing traders unless they are mathematically gifted like Alma. All the big shots who made it on wallstreet were math pros. For the rest of us, leaps, long term holds wins out. 

Heck the average joe who just bought the dip in April vs super educated hedge funds made more money this year or even since Covid. Buy the dip has trounced every other play :) Sad but true.
